// Fix for miscompilation: preserve select instructions when range metadata
// could be violated to avoid introducing poison where original code would not.

#include "InstCombineInternal.h"
#include "llvm/ADT/STLExtras.h"
#include "llvm/ADT/SmallVector.h"
#include "llvm/Analysis/InstructionSimplify.h"
#include "llvm/Analysis/ValueTracking.h"
#include "llvm/IR/IntrinsicInst.h"
#include "llvm/IR/PatternMatch.h"

using namespace llvm;
using namespace PatternMatch;

/// Check if simplifying a select instruction involving ctpop with range metadata
/// would introduce poison where the original code would not.
static bool isSafeToSimplifyCtpopSelect(SelectInst *SI) {
  // Pattern: select (icmp eq X, 0), true, (icmp eq (ctpop X), 1)
  Value *Cond = SI->getCondition();
  Value *TrueVal = SI->getTrueValue();
  Value *FalseVal = SI->getFalseValue();
  
  // Check if true value is constant true
  if (!match(TrueVal, m_One()))
    return true; // Not our pattern, safe to proceed with normal optimization
  
  // Check if condition is (icmp eq X, 0)
  ICmpInst *CondCmp = dyn_cast<ICmpInst>(Cond);
  if (!CondCmp || CondCmp->getPredicate() != ICmpInst::ICMP_EQ)
    return true;
  
  Value *X = nullptr;
  if (!match(CondCmp->getOperand(0), m_Value(X)) || 
      !match(CondCmp->getOperand(1), m_Zero()))
    return true;
  
  // Check if false value is (icmp eq (ctpop X), 1)
  ICmpInst *FalseCmp = dyn_cast<ICmpInst>(FalseVal);
  if (!FalseCmp || FalseCmp->getPredicate() != ICmpInst::ICMP_EQ)
    return true;
  
  Value *CtpopCall = FalseCmp->getOperand(0);
  ConstantInt *OneConst = dyn_cast<ConstantInt>(FalseCmp->getOperand(1));
  if (!OneConst || !OneConst->isOne())
    return true;
  
  // Check if CtpopCall is actually a ctpop intrinsic call with operand X
  IntrinsicInst *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(CtpopCall);
  if (!II || II->getIntrinsicID() != Intrinsic::ctpop || II->getOperand(0) != X)
    return true;
  
  // Now check if the ctpop call has range metadata that excludes 0

// Fix for miscompilation with ctpop range metadata
#include "InstCombineInternal.h"
#include "llvm/IR/IntrinsicInst.h"
#include "llvm/IR/PatternMatch.h"

using namespace llvm;
using namespace PatternMatch;

Instruction *llvm::InstCombiner::visitSelectInst(SelectInst &SI) {
  // Prevent unsafe optimization of select instructions involving ctpop with range metadata
  // that excludes 0, as this can introduce poison where the original code would not.
  
  Value *Cond = SI.getCondition();
  Value *TrueVal = SI.getTrueValue();
  Value *FalseVal = SI.getFalseValue();
  
  // Check if this matches the problematic pattern
  if (match(TrueVal, m_One())) {
    if (ICmpInst *CondCmp = dyn_cast<ICmpInst>(Cond)) {
      if (CondCmp->getPredicate() == ICmpInst::ICMP_EQ) {
        Value *X = CondCmp->getOperand(0);
        if (match(CondCmp->getOperand(1), m_Zero())) {
          if (ICmpInst *FalseCmp = dyn_cast<ICmpInst>(FalseVal)) {
            if (FalseCmp->getPredicate() == ICmpInst::ICMP_EQ) {
              if (IntrinsicInst *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(FalseCmp->getOperand(0))) {
                if (II->getIntrinsicID() == Intrinsic::ctpop && II->getOperand(0) == X) {
                  if (match(FalseCmp->getOperand(1), m_One())) {
                    // Check for range metadata excluding 0
                    if (MDNode *Range = II->getMetadata(LLVMContext::MD_range)) {
                      if (Range->getNumOperands() >= 2) {
                        if (ConstantInt *Low = mdconst::extract<ConstantInt>(Range->getOperand(0))) {
                          if (Low->getZExtValue() > 0) {
                            return nullptr; // Skip optimization to avoid poison
                          }
                        }
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  return nullptr;
}
</output>

// Fix for miscompilation with ctpop range metadata
#include "InstCombineInternal.h"
#include "llvm/IR/IntrinsicInst.h"
#include "llvm/IR/PatternMatch.h"

using namespace llvm;
using namespace PatternMatch;

Instruction *llvm::InstCombiner::visitSelectInst(SelectInst &SI) {
  // Prevent unsafe optimization of select instructions involving ctpop with range metadata
  // that excludes 0, as this can introduce poison where the original code would not.
  
  Value *Cond = SI.getCondition();
  Value *TrueVal = SI.getTrueValue();
  Value *FalseVal = SI.getFalseValue();
  
  // Check if this matches the problematic pattern
  if (match(TrueVal, m_One())) {
    if (ICmpInst *CondCmp = dyn_cast<ICmpInst>(Cond)) {
      if (CondCmp->getPredicate() == ICmpInst::ICMP_EQ) {
        Value *X = CondCmp->getOperand(0);
        if (match(CondCmp->getOperand(1), m_Zero())) {
          if (ICmpInst *FalseCmp = dyn_cast<ICmpInst>(FalseVal)) {
            if (FalseCmp->getPredicate() == ICmpInst::ICMP_EQ) {
              if (IntrinsicInst *II = dyn_cast<IntrinsicInst>(FalseCmp->getOperand(0))) {
                if (II->getIntrinsicID() == Intrinsic::ctpop && II->getOperand(0) == X) {
                  if (match(FalseCmp->getOperand(1), m_One())) {
                    // Check for range metadata excluding 0
                    if (MDNode *Range = II->getMetadata(LLVMContext::MD_range)) {
                      if (Range->getNumOperands() >= 2) {
                        if (ConstantInt *Low = mdconst::extract<ConstantInt>(Range->getOperand(0))) {
                          if (Low->getZExtValue() > 0) {
                            return nullptr; // Skip optimization to avoid poison
                          }
                        }
                      }
                    }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  return nullptr;
}
